# Knowledge Document Schema

## Pitch

Every knowledge document across the JOS portfolio uses this schema.
The schema makes documents first-class governed artifacts (per
JOS-C48): they carry their own identity, version, freshness signal,
provenance, and supersession links. Chunks (post-ingestion) become
derived artifacts — the document is the unit of truth.

## Required frontmatter

```yaml
---
# Universal identity
asset_type: kb-article | sop | playbook | filler | standard | template | reference
domain: <venture-specific-domain-id>   # e.g., domain-01-amazon for Silk
asset_id: <venture-prefix>-<asset-type-prefix>-<NNNN>
title: <human-readable title>
version: "MAJOR.MINOR"   # semantic; bumps on substantive content change
status: draft | active | superseded | retired
created: YYYY-MM-DD
updated: YYYY-MM-DD
owner: <person-or-team>

# Coverage (per Silk slot-map pattern)
covers_slots: [slot-id-1, slot-id-2, ...]   # standard slots this doc covers

# Tier-1 Discoverability (per Silk 2026-05-14)
last_verified: YYYY-MM-DD
last_verified_by: <person>
source_urls:
  - <url1>
  - <url2>
freshness_sla_days: 90   # default; per-domain override allowed

# Tier-2 Structural (per Silk SILK-D-014)
disposition: human | AI | hybrid | vendor
dependencies:
  must:
    - id: <other-asset-id>
      reason: <why required>
  should:
    - id: <other-asset-id>
      reason: <why recommended>
  can:
    - id: <other-asset-id>
      reason: <when useful>
exceptions:
  - condition: <when this doc doesn't apply>
    workaround: <what to do instead>
vendor: <vendor-name>   # only for filler docs that wrap a specific vendor's approach

# KB-specific
category: <category-label>
kb_tier: general | client-specific
china_panel_status: panel-reviewed | not-yet-reviewed | not-applicable   # for Silk
volatility: stable | evolving | experimental
---
```

## Field-by-field semantics

### Universal identity

- **`asset_type`** — the kind of knowledge doc. Common values
  inherited from Silk's content-architecture-contract:
  `kb-article` (Knowledge Base article), `sop` (SOP), `playbook`
  (strategic workflow), `filler` (proven tool for a specific slot),
  `standard` (slot map for a domain), `template` (scaffold), `reference`
  (saved external resource).
- **`domain`** — venture-specific domain ID. Ventures declare their
  domains in charter `knowledge_base:` block (per JOSW2 Group F
  Item 45). Silk has 10 domains (`domain-01-amazon`, etc.); Rocky
  Ridge has its own; other ventures declare theirs.
- **`asset_id`** — venture-scoped unique ID. Pattern:
  `<venture-prefix>-<asset-type-prefix>-<NNNN>`. E.g.,
  `SILK-KB-0042`, `RR-SOP-0007`.
- **`title`** — human-readable; this is what shows in INDEX MD files
  + CIP search results.
- **`version`** — semantic `MAJOR.MINOR`. Major bump for substantive
  shifts; minor for refinements.
- **`status`** — `draft` (in-progress), `active` (current canonical),
  `superseded` (newer version exists; this is retained for
  history), `retired` (no longer applicable).
- **`owner`** — person or team responsible for keeping this doc
  fresh.

### Coverage

- **`covers_slots`** — array of standard-slot IDs this doc covers.
  Connects the doc to the venture's content-architecture (per S35).
  Multiple slots allowed when the doc spans concerns.

### Tier-1 Discoverability

- **`last_verified`** — date a human or trusted-agent confirmed the
  doc's content matches reality. Freshness check uses this against
  `freshness_sla_days`.
- **`last_verified_by`** — who did the verification.
- **`source_urls`** — array of URLs the content cites. Provenance
  for fact-checking.
- **`freshness_sla_days`** — how many days before the doc is
  considered stale. Default 90; per-domain override via venture
  charter. Past SLA → drift warning in compliance-sweep.

### Tier-2 Structural

- **`disposition`** — who does this work: `human` (humans only),
  `AI` (agent-executable), `hybrid` (both involved), `vendor` (a
  specific external vendor's approach).
- **`dependencies.must / should / can`** — typed dependency arrays
  to other docs. Resolver validates these resolve to real
  `asset_id`s.
- **`exceptions`** — when this doc doesn't apply + the fallback.
- **`vendor`** — vendor name when `disposition: vendor`.

### KB-specific

- **`category`** — secondary classification within domain.
- **`kb_tier`** — `general` (applies broadly) or `client-specific`
  (tied to a particular client/customer). Drives CIP access scope.
- **`china_panel_status`** — Silk-specific provenance for content
  flowing through China-based supplier panels. Reusable for any
  venture with a similar review process; optional otherwise.
- **`volatility`** — `stable` (rarely changes), `evolving`
  (changes often), `experimental` (try at your own risk). Drives
  `freshness_sla_days` defaults.

## Cross-tenant / cross-domain rules

- `tenant_id` is NOT in the frontmatter — it's set by CIP at
  ingestion based on which venture owns the doc.
- `domain` value must be declared in the venture's charter
  `knowledge_base.domains[]` (per Item 45). Documents referencing
  undeclared domains fail the knowledge-doc-frontmatter gate.
- `dependencies.must / should / can` references can cite docs in
  OTHER domains within the same venture; cross-venture references
  require Tim's approval (handled by future bridge).

## How to extract YOUR knowledge document structure

When a piece of knowledge surfaces in a venture's WORKBENCH:

1. Confirm it's operating-surface knowledge (per JOS-C47). Build-time
   research stays in WORKBENCH.
2. Pick `asset_type` from the enum. If it doesn't fit, escalate via
   outbox (propose extension to the enum).
3. Pick `domain` from venture's declared list.
4. Allocate next `asset_id` in the type's sequence.
5. Verify with the venture lead before promoting.

## How to write a knowledge document well

- **One concept per doc.** If you're explaining 3 things, write 3 docs
  with dependencies between them.
- **State the conclusion in the first paragraph.** Readers (especially
  agents) need the headline answer fast.
- **Use the 8-section body format** (per Silk + S35 conventions):
  Overview → How it Works in Practice → Tools/Best Practices →
  Pitfalls → Alternatives → Cost/Timeline → Evidence/Calibration →
  References.
- **Date your claims.** If something is true as of Q2 2026, say so.
  Knowledge ages.
- **Cite sources in `source_urls`.** Future verifiers need them.
- **Update `last_verified` when you re-check.** Otherwise the
  freshness signal lies.

## Connected Objects

- **[JOS-C48]** — Knowledge as First-Class Artifact (foundation)
- **[JOS-S35]** — Knowledge-Organization Standard (sibling — domains
  + 5-level maturity)
- **[JOS-METHOD-AUTHOR-A-KNOWLEDGE-DOCUMENT]** — author procedure
- **[JOS-METHOD-INGEST-VENTURE-KB-INTO-CIP]** — ingestion procedure
- **[JOS-GATE-knowledge-doc-frontmatter]** — validation gate
- **(JOSW2 Group F Item 45)** — Charter `knowledge_base:` block
  declares domains + CIP source_ids per venture
- **(JOSW2 Group F Item 41)** — `foundry-cip-as-canonical-kb-host`
  decision (forthcoming)

## Drift-prevention

- **Don't omit `last_verified`.** Without it, docs rot silently in
  retrieval.
- **Don't reference undeclared domains.** Add the domain to charter
  first.
- **Don't bypass version bump on substantive change.** Readers track
  changes via version.
- **Don't author venture-side schemas that diverge.** Schema evolution
  goes through outbox.
- **Don't treat chunks as governable.** Chunks are derived; the
  document is the unit (per JOS-C48).
